stories.title: Trump makes case for Iran war. And, SCOTUS leans toward upholding birthright citizenship
stories.description: President Trump addressed the nation last night, making his case for war with Iran. And, the Supreme Court majority seemed inclined to rule against the Trump administration on birthright citizenship.

stories.title: Iran continues strikes across the Persian Gulf despite Trump's warning
stories.description: Iran continued to target Gulf countries with ballistic missiles and drones Thursday as the U.S. Embassy in Baghdad issued a security alert warning of attacks by Iran-backed militias.

stories.title: Pakistan is playing intermediary in the Iran war, a role it has played before
stories.description: Pakistan has emerged as a key intermediary in the U.S-Israel war with Iran. It played this role before, during a high-stakes moment in diplomatic history.

stories.title: U.S. lifts sanctions on Venezuela's acting President Delcy Rodríguez
stories.description: The newly announced sanctions relief is the latest U.S. recognition of Rodríguez as a legitimate authority in Venezuela ever since the U.S. military captured her predecessor, Nicolás Maduro.

stories.title: Who is held accountable if a war crime is committed in Iran?
stories.description: NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with international law expert Gabor Rona about attacks on civilian infrastructure in the Iran conflict and how they may constitute war crimes.

stories.title: A Turkish border town known for its cats - in times of peace
stories.description: The border town of Van in Turkey is known for being a hub for Iranian travelers - and its cats.

stories.title: Is the U.S. Navy ready to clear sea mines in the Persian Gulf?
stories.description: Despite the danger of sea mines, experts say that mine clearing has received minimal attention and funding from the U.S. Navy — and it's often overshadowed by more high-profile weapons systems.

stories.title: Trump says Iran war objectives 'nearing completion' in address to nation
stories.description: The US president's first televised address of the conflict with Iran did little to quell uneasy markets.

stories.title: Trump leaves key questions unanswered as he seeks to calm nerves over Iran war
stories.description: There were some glaring omissions in the president's primetime address, writes the BBC's Gary O'Donoghue.

stories.title: Iranian Nobel laureate suffered suspected heart attack in prison, family says
stories.description: Narges Mohammadi's brother says he fears her life is in imminent danger and she needs to be transferred to a hospital.

stories.title: Israel's perpetual war with Iran may be hard to win with military might alone
stories.description: The war with Iran has not yet resolved any of Israel's regional conflicts in the way its prime minister suggested it would.

stories.title: Israel intensifies Lebanon attacks and hits areas not in Hezbollah's control
stories.description: Attacks have continued as Israel has announced its intention to control swathes of south Lebanon.

stories.title: 'You're no longer my sister' - rows erupt as war divides Iranian families
stories.description: People in Iran describe angry scenes and tense relationships as rifts open up over the war.

stories.title: China is trying to play peacemaker in the Iran war - will it work?
stories.description: It's a turnaround for Beijing, whose official response has been muted so far. Why is China stepping in now?

stories.title: US journalist Shelly Kittleson kidnapped in Baghdad
stories.description: The US state department says a suspect in her abduction has ties to an Iran-backed militia group, Kataib Hezbollah.

stories.title: Israel says it will keep control over part of southern Lebanon after war with Hezbollah ends
stories.description: Defence Minister Israel Katz also says houses in Lebanese villages near the Israeli border will be demolished.

stories.title: Iran using children in security roles in war, reports and witnesses say
stories.description: An 11-year-old is reported to have been killed in an air strike while manning a checkpoint in Tehran.

stories.title: Latin Patriarch will have access to Jerusalem holy site after police stopped entry
stories.description: The Israeli PM says Cardinal Pierbattista Pizzaballa had been asked not to enter the church over concerns about Iranian strikes.

stories.title: Palestinian man killed as death toll from West Bank settler violence climbs
stories.description: Mohammad al-Malhi is the seventh Palestinian killed by a settler in a surge of violence since the start of the war with Iran.

stories.title: Three Lebanese journalists killed in Israeli strike, say broadcasters
stories.description: The Israeli military confirms it killed Ali Shoeib and Mohamed Ftouni, alleging they also belonged to Hezbollah's military wing.

stories.title: 'A million things could go wrong' - why seizing Iran's uranium would be so risky for the US
stories.description: Seizing the stockpile would be one of the "most complicated special operations in history", a former defence official tells the BBC.

stories.title: How Pakistan won over Trump to become an unlikely mediator in the Iran war
stories.description: Pakistan's role as intermediary in this conflict took many by surprise. But perhaps it shouldn't have.

stories.title: Middle East war creates multi-faceted challenge for airlines
stories.description: Global air travel remains ⁠severely disrupted, with many people ​still unable to fly as planned to destinations after the Iran war forced the closure of major Middle Eastern hubs, including Dubai, Doha and Abu Dhabi.

stories.title: 'People are exhausted': No end in sight as Lebanon enters second month of war
stories.description: Lebanese Prime Minister Nawaf Salam said on Thursday there was no end in sight to a ​war that had already displaced a million people over the last month, as families fleeing Israeli strikes said they were exhausted by repeated rounds of conflict. Lebanon is entering the second mo...

stories.title: Britain says 40 countries discuss reopening Strait of Hormuz after Iran blockade
stories.description: About 40 countries are discussing joint action to reopen the Strait of Hormuz to stop Iran holding "the global economy hostage," Britain said on Thursday, after US President Donald Trump said securing the waterway was ​for others to ‌resolve.

stories.title: Macron says 'unrealistic' to open Hormuz Strait by force, urges Trump to 'be serious'
stories.description: French President Emmanuel Macron on Thursday said it would be unrealistic to launch a military operation to force open the Strait of Hormuz after US President Donald Trump challenged allies to work towards reopening it. In unusually strong criticism, Macron accused Trump of const...

stories.title: US war on Iran: Reactions in Israel as Trump’s address 'barely' mention ally
stories.description: Iran responded to US President Donald Trump’s address to Americans on the war with new missile attacks targeting Israel and the Gulf Arab states Thursday, underlining Tehran’s insistence that it rejected Washington’s outreach for a ceasefire while maintaining its grip on the Stra...

stories.title: ‘Back to the Stone Ages’: #Trump says #Iran war nearly over in national #address
stories.description: US President Donald Trump delivered his first nationwide address since the start of the war in the Middle East on Wednesday. Trump claimed the US was “very close” to achieving its objectives in Iran and vowed to hit the country “extremely hard” in the coming days.

stories.title: Takeaways from Trump's address: No end date for Iran war and few details on strategy ahead
stories.description: President Donald Trump sought Wednesday to explain his rationale for the war against Iran at a pivotal moment at home and abroad, but he offered few new details. Notably missing from Trump’s primetime address was his oft-repeated assertion that negotiations with Iran were underwa...

stories.title: Critical aid supplies to Sudan disrupted by 'ripple effect' from Middle East war
stories.description: Critical aid supplies to Sudan are being disrupted by the 'ripple effect' of the Iran war, the World Food Program (WFP) warned on Wednesday. While gas, oil and fertilisers are stuck in the Persian Gulf due to the war, urgent food aid destined to Sudan and other countries are faci...

stories.title: Iran vows 'more destructive' attacks after Trump threats
stories.description: Iran on Thursday threatened "crushing" attacks on the US and Israel, firing missiles at Tel Aviv after US President Donald Trump vowed to bomb the Islamic republic "back to the Stone Ages". The war, which erupted more than a month ago with US-Israeli strikes on Iran, has spread t...

stories.title: Iran sets up 'tollbooth' in Strait of Hormuz for ships seeking safe passage
stories.description: Oil prices rose and stocks fell on Wednesday following US President Donald Trump’s speech in which he warned he would hit Iran “extremely hard” over the next two to three weeks. Meanwhile, Iran has reportedly begun collecting tolls from ships seeking safe passage through the Horm...

stories.title: UK-led coalition of 40 countries vows action on Hormuz Strait gridlock
stories.description: UK PM Keir Starmer urges non-military solutions ahead of coalition meeting to ease gridlock in vital Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Pakistan to continue with Iran-US mediation despite ‘obstacles’
stories.description: Islamabad's statement follows Trump threatening to bomb Iran 'back to the Stone Ages'.

stories.title: Smoke rises over Mashhad, Iran, following strike near airport
stories.description: Footage shows columns of smoke rising over the Iranian city of Mashhad, after US-Israeli strikes near the airport.

stories.title: Pakistan-bound ships pass through Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: Two ships have passed through the Strait of Hormuz and arrived at Karachi Port.

stories.title: Israeli strikes kill 7 in southern Lebanon, Hezbollah targets ground troops
stories.description: Lebanon's prime minister warns of a 'critical' situation as Israel says it is working to dismantle Hezbollah.

stories.title: Yemen’s Houthis coordinate with Iran, but retain independence, despite war
stories.description: Houthi actions align with Iran’s strategy, but maintain autonomy to maximise regional influence and domestic objectives.

stories.title: US-Israeli attacks and its impact on Iran’s economy
stories.description: US-Israeli attacks and its impact on Iran’s economy

stories.title: Iran’s ex-foreign minister Kharazi ‘gravely wounded’ in attack on his home
stories.description: Apparent assassination attempt comes as Iran's army pledges to fight until the US, Israel face 'permanent surrender'.

stories.title: Oil price surges, Asian stocks fall as Trump vows to continue Iran attacks
stories.description: Asian stocks also took a hit after Trump’s speech as most Southeast Asian countries rely heavily on oil imports.

stories.title: UK to host 35-state moot on reopening of Hormuz Strait
stories.description: LONDON: Britain will this week hold a meeting of about 35 countries to discuss how to reopen the strategic Strait of Hormuz which has been crippled by the Middle East war, Prime Minister Keir Starmer announced on Wednesday. British Foreign Secretary Yvette Cooper will host the di...

stories.title: Graveyard raid in India uncovers hidden cooking gas canisters amid shortage
stories.description: Indian police this week seized 414 cooking‑gas canisters hidden in a graveyard in Hyderabad city and arrested those involved in trying to ​sell them on the black market amid shortages due to the ‌US-Israeli war on Iran, a government official said on Thursday. Authorities have ste...

stories.title: Nasa launches 4 astronauts on world’s first crewed lunar mission in half a century
stories.description: Four astronauts blasted off from Florida on Wednesday on Nasa’s Artemis II mission, a high-stakes voyage around the moon that marks the United States’ boldest step yet toward returning humans to the lunar surface later this decade in a race with China. Nasa’s Space Launch System ...

stories.title: US reconnaissance drone detected off Iran coast calls Italian base home
stories.description: The UAV, which has been repeatedly detected in this area, including near the city of Bushehr, has been in the air for multiple hours and is operating at an altitude above 14.5 km, according to the source

stories.title: Austria denies US warplanes access to its airspace for Iran war — radio
stories.description: According to the report, the decision stems from a national law on neutrality

stories.title: Iran’s elite army unit dismisses US back-to-Stone-Age threats as Hollywood fantasies
stories.description: Earlier, the US leader asserted that Iran’s Navy and Army have been obliterated, while the Islamic republic’s missile capabilities will soon be reduced to a minimum

stories.title: IDF eliminates 40 radicals, strikes dozens of Hezbollah sites in Lebanon over past day
stories.description: The Israeli Navy conducted a precise strike targeting a Hezbollah weapons storage facility in southern Lebanon

stories.title: Russian MP warns of nuclear proliferation risk if Israel uses nukes against Iran
stories.description: Alexey Pushkov noted that the question of whether Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu will use nuclear weapons in Iran has been actively discussed on American television in recent days

stories.title: Iran’s parliament speaker says 7 million Iranians ready to defend their country with arms
stories.description: On March 29, US President Donald Trump announced that US troops may as well seize Kharg Island

stories.title: AI takes center stage in Iranian conflict — Russian parliamentarian
stories.description: According to Alexey Pushkov, the first thousand targets hit in Iran were designated by artificial intelligence through a special system

stories.title: Western plans for Iran have failed — Iranian government
stories.description: Head of the government's Information Council Elias Hazrati stressed that the Iranian people displayed unprecedented unity

stories.title: Russia pushes for ending aggression against Iran that affects Persian countries — MFA
stories.description: Russian Deputy Foreign Minister Georgy Borisenko earlier held a phone call with his Saudi counterpart, Waleed bin Abdulkarim El-Khereiji

stories.title: Iranian MFA confirms US uses PrSM missile with tungsten pellets in strike on sports hall
stories.description: "This is a despicable war crime," Esmaeil Baghaei said

stories.title: IN BRIEF: Kremlin statements on US gameplan in Iran
stories.description: Dmitry Peskov said the current situation in the Middle East should be "shepherded to the peace track" as soon as possible

stories.title: Iran attacks US steel plants in UAE, Bahrain, strikes Israel's Rafael plant
stories.description: The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps stated that the strike had been launched in response to attacks on Iranian industry which resulted in the deaths and injuries of a number of workers

stories.title: Iran threatens more powerful strikes against Israeli, US infrastructure in Mideast
stories.description: The IRGC earlier announced strikes against a United States Steel plant in Abu Dhabi

stories.title: Iran has shot down 154 US, Israeli drones since conflict began — military
stories.description: According to the army press service, several hours ago, Iranian air defenses successfully intercepted two MQ-9 drones

stories.title: Strikes on Iran's Bushehr NPP violate international law — permanent rep in Vienna
stories.description: Reza Najafi emphasized that even in wartime, it remained illegal to strike targets used by the civilian population

stories.title: DPRK slams U.S. for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang
stories.description: The Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK) has slammed the United States for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang.
